prompt.content:
Use file “Onc_Patient 7” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

-----

The patient presents to her oncologist to re-establish care after a few years. Refer to the reference files "Onc_Patient 7", "Onc_Patient 7 script.pdf", and “Labs.pdf”. Assume the patient is 90 years old; please ignore the patient's other age in the patient’s chart. 

What is the next best systemic treatment option for this patient? Please provide the clinical rationale. Answers should align with the most current clinical guidelines as of January 10, 2026.

extra.task_name: Web Search_Attachments_Onc Patient 7 - ERBB2 mutation - Eliane

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
The patient has a history of stage I non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). Previously, they received pembrolizumab and chemotherapy with carboplatin/pemetrexed or cisplatin. Currently, there is evidence of brain metastases, and a lymph node biopsy indicates recurrent NSCLC with an A775_G776insYVMA (HER2) mutation on exon 20.

For the next line of systemic therapy, options include fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki, zongertinib, and sevabertinib. Zongertinib specifically targets the A775_G776insYVMA mutation and shows notable efficacy against brain metastases. It also carries the lowest risk of drug-induced pneumonitis and does not contraindicate use in patients with autoimmune diseases.

Therefore, zongertinib is recommended as the most suitable next systemic treatment for this patient.
